page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. This ARMv8 64-bit architecture features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results place the c6gd.2xlarge in the average tier for multi-core operations and memory bandwidth. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 12133.14 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5364.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 473.11 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 116.89 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with a Geekbench single-core score of 930.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression speed of 6.93 GB/sec, both falling into the lower percentiles.

Given its dedicated ARM cores and integrated NVMe storage, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that benefit from local scratch space and parallel processing without requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, as demonstrated by its 9.78 GB/sec static web throughput and 9.43 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Conversely, it is poorly suited for single-threaded tasks, heavy cryptography, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores (8 vCPUs) running at 2.5 GHz, 16.0 GB of memory, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12133.14, alongside high memory read bandwidth. However, single-core performance is limited, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 930.0. This instance provides a cost-effective hardware profile for parallelizable workloads that require local high-speed storage, such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive cryptographic operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 474 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core and database performance.

16.0 GB of system memory paired with 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
